_meta.title: DD Form 1380 Field Inventory
primary_sources: Casualty Documentation DD1380 / chapter 2 — INSTRUCTIONS FOR USING DD FORM 1380 (JTS/CoTCCC verbatim)

section_overlay.A: Front header: Battle Roster, EVAC, Name, Last 4, Gender, Date, Time, Service, Unit, Allergies
section_overlay.B: Mechanism of Injury, body diagram, four TQ corners (R Arm, L Arm, R Leg, L Leg)
section_overlay.D: Back header: Battle Roster + EVAC repeated from front
section_overlay.E: Treatments: C(TQ + Dressing), A(Airway), B(Breathing), C(Fluid + Blood Product)
section_overlay.F: MEDS (Analgesic, Antibiotic, Other) + OTHER row (Combat-Pill-Pack, Eye-Shield, Splint, Hypothermia-Prevention)

domain_notes: Battle Roster format: 2014-era policy specifies last-4-of-SSN, but current field convention uses last-4-of-DoD-ID-number (post-SSN-phase-out). Also called ZAP number or Kill Card Number. Per-unit SOP may differ. The regex validates format only — initials + 4 digits — agnostic to which 4 digits.
AVPU on the printed form has dropdown indicators next to each cell. Allowed values are A, V, P, U.

The form has up to 4 columns of vital sign readings. Each column is a timestamped snapshot. The grid is modeled here as one row per vital, with an implicit array structure for the 4 timestamped readings.

fields.format_constraints: First initial + last initial + last four of DoD ID number (formerly last four of SSN per 2014 policy). Per-unit SOP may differ. Also known as ZAP number or Kill Card Number. Convention only — not standardized DoD-wide.
Mark an X on the casualty's evacuation priority/precedence. Urgent = evac <1 hr. Priority = evac <4 hrs. Routine = evac <24 hrs.
Last, First.
Last four numbers of casualty's Social Security number (2014 policy) or DoD ID number (current convention).
Mark an X for Male (M) or Female (F).
Date of injury in DD-MMM-YR format. Example: 29-JUN-13.
24-hour time of injury, suffixed with L (local) or Z (zulu). Example: 1300Z.
Casualty's branch of service (USA, USAF, USCG, USN, USMC). For U.S. civilians, write US CIV. For non-U.S. personnel, write NON US or a standard abbreviation for casualty's nationality.
Casualty's unit name.
Casualty's known drug allergies. If no drug allergies, write NKDA (no known drug allergies).

Mark an X on the mechanism or cause of injury. Mark all that apply. If multiple mechanisms of injury and multiple injuries, draw a line between the mechanism of injury and the anatomical site of the injury.
Free text describing the mechanism of injury when 'Other' is selected.
Mark an X at the site of the injury(ies) on the body picture (front and back silhouettes). For burn injuries, circle the burn percentage(s) on the figure.
If a tourniquet is applied to the right arm, write type of tourniquet used.
Time of tourniquet application. Same time format convention as the casualty's injury time.
If a tourniquet is applied to the left arm, write type of tourniquet used.
Time of tourniquet application.
If a tourniquet is applied to the right leg, write type of tourniquet used.
Time of tourniquet application.
If a tourniquet is applied to the left leg, write type of tourniquet used.
Time of tourniquet application.
Time of vital sign reading. The grid supports up to 4 timestamped column readings.
Pulse rate and anatomical location (e.g., 110 radial, weak carotid).
Systolic / Diastolic. Pre-printed slash on the form.
Breaths per minute.
Oxygen saturation percentage.

Level of consciousness. A = Alert, V = responds to Verbal stimulus, P = responds to Pain stimulus, U = Unresponsive. Required to be documented prior to administering ketamine per 2026 TCCC Guidelines section 11.
Numeric rating scale. 0 = no pain, 10 = worst pain.
Same format as front-of-card Battle Roster. Repeated on back of card for continuity if cards are separated.
Same as front-of-card EVAC. Repeated on back of card for continuity.
Mark X for Circulation hemorrhage control TQ category.
Write name of TQ(s) used (e.g., CAT, SOFTT-W).
Mark X for dressing category.
Write name of dressing(s) used (e.g., Combat Gauze, Celox, ChitoGauze, XStat, iTClamp).
Mark X for Airway interventions. Intact = no intervention required. NPA = nasopharyngeal airway. CRIC = cricothyroidotomy. ET-Tube = endotracheal tube. SGA = supraglottic airway.
Write type of airway device(s) used.
Mark X for Breathing interventions. O2 = oxygen. Needle-D = needle decompression. Chest-Tube = thoracostomy tube. Chest-Seal = chest seal (vented preferred per 2026 guidelines).
Write type of breathing device(s) used.
Circulation resuscitation. Repeating row with columns: Name, Volume, Route, Time. Multiple fluid entries allowed per casualty.

Circulation resuscitation. Repeating row with columns: Name, Volume, Route, Time. Per 2026 guidelines order-of-preference: cold-stored low-titer O whole blood, pre-screened low-titer O fresh whole blood, plasma+RBC+platelets 1:1:1, plasma+RBC 1:1, plasma alone, RBCs alone.

Repeating row: Name, Dose, Route, Time. Examples on form: Ketamine, Fentanyl, Morphine. Per 2026 CWMP: Acetaminophen 1000-1300mg PO q8h, Meloxicam 15mg PO QD, Suzetrigine 100mg PO once then 50mg PO q12h.
Repeating row: Name, Dose, Route, Time. Examples on form: Moxifloxacin, Ertapenem. Per 2026 guidelines: Cefadroxil 1g PO QD if able PO; Ceftriaxone 2g IV/IO/IM QD if unable PO.
Repeating row: Name, Dose, Route, Time. Example on form: TXA. Per 2026 guidelines: 2g TXA via slow IV/IO push if hemorrhagic shock or significant TBI, NOT later than 3 hours after injury.
Mark X if combat pill pack (CWMP) administered.
Mark X if eye shield applied. Mark right (R) or left (L) sub-checkbox. Both may be marked for bilateral eye injury.
Mark X if splint applied. Type of splint (SAM, traction, Sager, improvised) goes in Notes if needed — the form does not have a dedicated splint Type field.
Mark X if hypothermia prevention measures applied.
Type of hypothermia-prevention device used (e.g., HPMK, Blizzard Survival Blanket, Heat-Reflective Shell, hooded sleeping bag with vapor barrier).
Record any other pertinent information and/or clarifications. If more space is needed, attach another DD Form 1380 to the original; label the second card #2 and include the casualty's name and unit.

Print the first responder's name (Last, First). Per JTS definition: medical or non-medical personnel who provided initial/immediate self-aid, buddy aid, point-of-injury care, and/or tactical field care.
Last four numbers of first responder's Social Security number (2014 policy) or DoD ID number (current convention).
